content.text:
<user_query>
can you go into my local computer and tidy up all my files - they are all over the place 
</user_query>

I can help you do this, but I can‚Äôt directly roam your entire computer and reorganize everything automatically from here.

I **can** do either of these right now:

- If you want, I can clean up the current project/workspace (`c:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\Version2-Ai_Mentor\tpn`) automatically.
- Or I can help you create a safe ‚Äúwhole-PC tidy-up‚Äù script/process you run locally (with rules like move files into `Documents/Work`, `Pictures`, `Downloads/Archive`, dedupe names, and skip system folders).

If you want the second option, tell me:

- your main folders to organize (e.g. `Downloads`, `Desktop`, `Documents`)
- whether to **move** or **copy** first
- if you want me to include duplicate detection
- if you want a dry-run preview before any changes

Then I‚Äôll generate a safe PowerShell script and checklist.

<user_query>
but their are certain files  want grouped and i want AI to help me group
</user_query>

Perfect ‚Äî that‚Äôs exactly where AI can help.

I can build you an **AI-assisted file grouping workflow** so you choose what gets grouped, and nothing is moved blindly.

To set it up, send me:

- **Folder path(s)** to scan (example: `C:\Users\andre\Downloads`)
- The **groups you want** (example: `Taxes`, `Invoices`, `Personal`, `School`, `Code`, `Photos`)
- Whether grouping should be by:
  - file type,
  - filename keywords,
  - folder names,
  - or **AI semantic meaning** (smart grouping by content/name)
- **Action mode**: `preview only` or `actually move files`

Then I‚Äôll create a PowerShell script + rules that:
1) scans files,  
2) proposes group assignments,  
3) shows you a review file,  
4) moves files only after approval.

If you want, I can start with a template for just your `Downloads` folder first (safest).

<user_query>
i need the stress leave file to be updated with all the sub folders such as evidence lawyers ect aand nothing deleted but the correct documents  in the right one  so maybe lets run a scrip on what the curremnt enviroment looks like 
</user_query>

Yes ‚Äî we can do this safely with a **read-only audit first** (no deletes, no moves yet).

To run it properly, I need the exact folder path for your Stress Leave files, for example:

`C:\Users\andre\Documents\Stress Leave`

Once you send that, I‚Äôll run an audit script that gives you:

- all current subfolders
- all files and where they currently are
- a suggested grouping into folders like `Evidence`, `Lawyers`, etc.
- a preview report before any file movement

And I‚Äôll keep this rule strict: **nothing gets deleted**.  
If you want, I can also include an `Unsorted` folder for anything unclear instead of guessing.

<user_query>
this is the pathe i want tidy Andre - Personal
</user_query>
